sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The purple UI button click caused a board change. Let me compare what changed between before and after. I need to look at the transition to see what actually changed.
Looking at the images, the state looks the same. The board_changed was True though. Let me check what changed more carefully.
The step counter went from 142 to 143, which is expected. But the actual gameplay objects seem unchanged. The top blocks are still white-on-top, purple-on-bottom.
Wait - let me look more carefully at the UI buttons. The right button (white) might have changed. Or maybe something subtle changed.
Let me compare the before and after frames to see what actually changed.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: Board changed but objects look the same visually. Let me check what actually changed by comparing before/after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after the purple button click
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']

print("BEFORE:")
for n in before:
    if n['pixels'] >= 30:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_row = min(r for r,c in b)
        min_col = min(c for r,c in b)
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash']}, pos=({min_row},{min_col})")

print("\nAFTER:")
for n in after:
    if n['pixels'] >= 30:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_row = min(r for r,c in b)
        min_col = min(c for r,c in b)
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash']}, pos=({min_row},{min_col})")

# Check for differences
before_hashes = {n['hash']: n for n in before}
after_hashes = {n['hash']: n for n in after}
print(f"\nBefore hashes: {set(before_hashes.keys())}")
print(f"After hashes: {set(after_hashes.keys())}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
